disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Loss of consciousness came on suddenly at the emotional peak of a heated argument, with rapid complete recovery in under a minute
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when you fainted?
How long were you out for, and how did you feel afterwards?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It happened right in the middle of a big shouting match — I was really worked up, my heart raced, and then I just went down. I was only out for a few seconds and woke up feeling okay.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid forceful palpitations immediately preceded the loss of consciousness
trigger_question_examples: Did you notice anything just before you passed out?
Was your heart doing anything unusual before you collapsed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah — right before I blacked out my heart was pounding really fast and hard, faster than I've ever felt it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent lightheadedness and near-fainting during hard exertion (competitive sport) over the past year
trigger_question_examples: Have you ever felt dizzy or close to passing out at other times, like during sport or exercise?
Has anything like this nearly happened before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, over the last year I've had a few times playing sport where I got really dizzy and lightheaded, like I might go down — but I never actually fainted until tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Family history of sudden unexplained death in a young relative during physical activity (maternal cousin collapsed and died at 16 while playing basketball)
trigger_question_examples: Has anyone in your family died suddenly or unexpectedly, especially when they were young?
Is there any history of heart problems or sudden death in the family?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My cousin on my mum's side died suddenly when he was 16 — he just dropped while playing basketball. No one really knew why.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid return to full baseline with no confusion, no tongue-biting, no jerking movements witnessed
trigger_question_examples: When you came round, were you confused or groggy?
Did anyone see any shaking, jerking, or did you bite your tongue?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I woke up straight away and knew where I was — no confusion. My parents didn't see any shaking and I didn't bite my tongue or wet myself.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain and no shortness of breath before, during, or after the event
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain or trouble breathing with it?
Did your chest hurt at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain and no trouble breathing — it was just the racing heart and then blacking out.
